steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
Factor by splitting the middle term. The values $35$ and $-4$ have product $-140$ and sum $31$:
$$5x^2+31x-28=5x^2+35x-4x-28$$
$$=5x(x+7)-4(x+7)$$
$$=(5x-4)(x+7)$$

steps.title: Compare the listed factors
steps.content: Statement I lists $x-7$, but the factorization contains $x+7$, so statement I is not a factor. Statement II lists $5x-4$, which appears in the factorization, so statement II is a factor.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
Choices that include statement I cannot work, and the choice that excludes statement II cannot work. Only the choice for statement II alone matches the factorization.
The answer is B.
